dealname: Mosaic Robotics -- Zara Okoye Relocation (Savannah to Atlanta) [RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM]
description:
RISK FLAG -- PROTOTYPE DAMAGE INCIDENT (Updated April 15, 2026 by Emeka Diallo)

Original deal: Relocation for Zara Okoye (Robotics Prototyping Engineer) from Savannah, GA to Atlanta, GA. Standard package ($1,520) + custom prototype crating and climate monitoring surcharge ($1,200) = $2,700 total.

INCIDENT SUMMARY:
On April 15, 2026, Zara Okoye inspected the delivered prototypes and discovered significant damage to Prototype Unit #1:
- Cracked servo motor housing (3-inch fracture along lateral face, internal wiring harness exposed)
- Joint calibration misalignment (4+ degrees drift on two axes, consistent with shock or sustained vibration)

Unit #1 is valued at $90,000. Prototype Unit #2 and control unit appear undamaged pending full diagnostic.

FINANCIAL EXPOSURE:
- Total prototype insured value: $180,000 (2 units)
- Damaged unit value: $90,000
- MoveOps vendor liability coverage: $50,000 per item
- UNCOVERED GAP: $40,000 -- this is MoveOps' direct financial exposure if Heartland Movers does not accept liability
- Related invoice: INV-2026-0411 ($9,010 for 3 relocations)
- Credit memo: CM-2026-0415 (insurance claim placeholder)
- Liability accrual: ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90K contingent liability booked)

CONTRIBUTING FACTORS:
1. Coordinator Blessing Okafor signed off on Heartland's packing checklist remotely (was in Philadelphia for concurrent Canopy Health move). Foam density not physically verified against 4-inch minimum spec.
2. Delivery receipt signed by Kyle Britten (Mosaic Robotics intern), not by Zara Okoye. No damage inspection at time of delivery. ~3.75 hours between delivery (9:15 AM) and Zara's inspection (~1:00 PM).
3. Heartland transit details (overnight stop, temperature logs) not yet confirmed.

ACCOUNT RISK:
HIGH -- Mosaic Robotics is one of MoveOps' newest client relationships (onboarded ~2 months ago). Tamara Byrd (HR Manager) has CC'd Rafael Mendes (VP of Engineering, executive sponsor of the prototype program) on the escalation. The damaged prototype is part of a DARPA-adjacent R&D program. A $40K-$90K loss on this claim would exceed 4x the total invoice revenue from this client. The incident report is due by EOD April 17. This claim could jeopardize the entire Mosaic account if not handled with full transparency and strong remediation.

Vendor: Heartland Movers | Coordinator: Blessing Okafor | Invoice: INV-2026-0411 | Contact: contact_mosaic_zara_okoye

description:
EXECUTIVE RELOCATION — FIRST C-SUITE PLACEMENT FOR CANOPY HEALTH

Origin: Boston, MA
Destination: Chicago, IL
Target on-site date: May 5, 2026
Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi
Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto
Estimate: EST-2026-0422 | $12,170 total | Due May 7, 2026 (Net-15)

Service Package (7 line items):
1. Apartment sourcing fee: $1,000
2. Temporary corporate housing, 4 weeks furnished 2BR Streeterville/River North via UrbanNest: $4,800
3. Chain-of-custody documentation surcharge (SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet, ~350 lbs, patient research data binders from 3 clinical trials, signed handoff log at origin and destination, direct transport only): $1,200
4. Specialty equipment crating x2 (Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated diagnostic monitors, S/N RX660-1147A and RX660-1203A, ~$12K each, anti-static packaging, calibration certificates must travel with units): $800
5. Household moving coordination, Boston to Chicago via Swift Relocations, full white-glove packing/unpacking, furniture disassembly/reassembly: $3,500
6. Flight booking with premium cabin upgrade (2 passengers, partner is 7 months pregnant): $370
7. White-glove executive service premium (dedicated coordinator, priority scheduling): $500

Special Considerations:
- Partner Priya Mehta is 7 months pregnant (due late June). All move logistics must minimize disruption and stress. Elevator-access housing, doorman building, proximity to Northwestern Prentice Women's Hospital for prenatal care.
- Origin is a third-floor walkup in Boston with no elevator — crew planning required for 350-lb filing cabinet.
- Canopy board-level visibility on this transition. First time Canopy has trusted MoveOps with a C-suite hire. Must be flawless.
- Housing options sourced from UrbanNest: 3 options presented to Dr. Mehta, recommendation is Streeterville (The Pinnacle, 400 E Ohio St, $1,350/week). Awaiting Dr. Mehta's selection.
- Chain-of-custody documentation template needed from Canopy compliance team — coordination with Patricia Langford pending.

STRATEGIC NOTE: This relocation represents a significant deepening of the Canopy Health relationship. They've been our longest-standing client since July 2024, but all previous relocations were mid-level staff (clinical data analysts, UX researchers, lab associates). Graduating to executive-level placements opens a new revenue tier. David Chen flagged this as a potential template for a formal Executive Relocation Package service offering.
